title: phebalium canaliculatum
intro: Phebalium canaliculatum, also known as the paperbark or stringybark, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Phebalium canaliculatum include its ability to soothe and calm digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin irritations. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
